description:
The literal one-line root cause of the `.29.12.1` "no covering spec
for Cont" panic. Immediate relief: no architectural change required.

## Verified state on main

ir_typer.rs:719:
```rust
match site.slot {
    EmitSlot::Direct | EmitSlot::ClosureLit(..) | EmitSlot::CallClosureKnown => {
        let cid = site.callsite_id();
        callsite_outcome_updates.insert(
            cid,
            CallsiteOutcome::Emitted {
                target: target.clone(),
                came_from: None,
            },
        );
    }
    EmitSlot::Cont | EmitSlot::MakeClosure(_) => {}   // <-- skipped
}
```

The walk DOES emit cont specs at ir_typer.rs:1517
(`emit(slot, b.id, (cont.fn_id, key), out)`). Only the
outcome-publishing step is missing.

ir_codegen.rs:3986 panics when `spec_registry.resolve` can't find a
covering spec for the cont — which happens when the typer's
flow-narrowed registered key doesn't match codegen's flow-narrowed
lookup key.

Repro that panics today (the fz-t45 ticket's program):
```fz
fn each(_, []), do: nil
fn each(f, [h | t]) do
  f(h)
  each(f, t)
end
fn main() do
  k = 10
  each(fn(x) -> print(x + k), [1, 2, 3])
end
```

## Fix

Move `EmitSlot::Cont` from the skip arm to the publish arm at
ir_typer.rs:719–729. Same shape as the other variants:
`CallsiteOutcome::Emitted { target, came_from: None }`.

## Decision: `EmitSlot::MakeClosure(_)`

Left in the skip arm for now. The closure body picker uses
`spec_registry.resolve` directly today; fz-rcp.3 + fz-rcp.5 together
will handle it via `naturally_opaque` + outcome read. If fz-rcp.5
needs MakeClosure outcomes published, lift them out of the skip arm
there. This ticket is scoped to Cont only — minimum change for
maximum panic relief.

## Independence

This ticket changes the typer only; codegen still calls
`spec_registry.resolve`. Codegen now finds the cont spec because
the typer already registered it in `mt.specs` — the panic stops
firing because the registry actually has the entry, not because
codegen reads outcomes (that's fz-rcp.5).

Wait — verify this is actually true before landing. The panic fires
when `spec_registry.resolve(cont.fn_id, &key)` returns None. If the
spec is registered, resolve finds it. Publishing the outcome makes
fz-rcp.5 possible but doesn't itself prevent the panic unless the
typer is also registering the missing spec. Investigate during
implementation: is the cont spec absent from the registry, or
present but not findable via the codegen lookup key?

If the cont spec is genuinely missing from `mt.specs`, this ticket
also needs to ensure the typer registers it (likely already does via
the walk; double-check). If it's present but the key differs, this
ticket is solving the wrong problem and the real fix is fz-rcp.5
(codegen reads outcomes, ignores key mismatch).

## Acceptance

- fz-t45's repro program compiles past the `.29.12.1` panic.
- Output may still be wrong (wire-ABI mismatch in TailCallClosure
  indirect path — separate issue, fz-t45 itself).
- 166-fixture matrix green.
- Investigation note in PR: which of the two scenarios above
  applied, and why this ticket's change is sufficient.
title: fz-rcp.4: typer publishes Emitted outcomes for EmitSlot::Cont — retire .29.12.1 panic
